name: Steel Mill Slab Design - CR3
content: Orders of the same colour should be kept together in the same slab whenever possible. If a colour appears on multiple slabs, each additional slab used by that colour incurs one unit of penalty. The aim is to minimize the total waste together with the total colour-splitting penalty.
value_info: `orders`: List of dicts, each with `size` (int) and `color` (int), representing customer orders.
`ref_opt_val`: Optional integer reference optimum for the combined objective; null until filled in.

var1.descr: `order_to_slab`: List[int] of length n_orders, where order_to_slab[i] is the slab index assigned to order i.

var2.descr: `slab_loads`: List[int] of length n_slabs, where slab_loads[j] is the total weight of orders assigned to slab j.

var3.descr: `slab_losses`: List[int] of length n_slabs, where slab_losses[j] is the wasted capacity on slab j.

var4.descr: `total_split_penalty`: Integer penalty for colours that are split across multiple slabs.

var5.descr: `total_loss`: Integer total wasted capacity across all slabs.

var6.descr: `total_objective`: Integer combined objective equal to total_loss + total_split_penalty.

complexity.difficulty_rationale: Adds helper-variable families for colour usage counts and split penalties, strengthens the colour-linking semantics, and changes the objective from pure waste minimization to a combined waste-plus-penalty objective.

cr_impact.notes: Adds reverse use_color linking (21 constraints), per-colour slab counts and split penalties (6 constraints), and total aggregation constraints (3 constraints). New variable families: color_slab_count, split_penalty, total_split_penalty, total_loss, total_objective.
